english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Galatians

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus, Apostolus non ab hominibus, neque per hominem, sed per Jesum Christum, et Deum Patrem, qui suscitavit eum a mortuis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος ἀπόστολος οὐκ ἀπ᾽ ἀνθρώπων οὐδὲ δι᾽ ἀνθρώπου ἀλλὰ διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ καὶ θεοῦ πατρὸς τοῦ ἐγείραντος αὐτὸν ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: Paul, an apostle, (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui mecum sunt omnes fratres, ecclesiis Galatiæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ σὺν ἐμοὶ πάντες ἀδελφοί ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς γαλατίας
verses.king_james: And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia vobis, et pax a Deo Patre, et Domino nostro Jesu Christo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χάρις ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς καὶ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Grace *be* to you and peace from God the Father, and *from* our Lord Jesus Christ,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui dedit semetipsum pro peccatis nostris, ut eriperet nos de præsenti sæculo nequam, secundum voluntatem Dei et Patris nostri,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ δόντος ἑαυτὸν ὑπὲρ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν ὅπως ἐξέληται ἡμᾶς ἐκ τοῦ ἐνεστῶτος αἰῶνος πονηροῦ κατὰ τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ πατρὸς ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:

verses.latin_vulgate: cui est gloria in sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων ἀμήν
verses.king_james: To whom *be* glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Miror quod sic tam cito transferimini ab eo qui vos vocavit in gratiam Christi in aliud Evangelium:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θαυμάζω ὅτι οὕτως ταχέως μετατίθεσθε ἀπὸ τοῦ καλέσαντος ὑμᾶς ἐν χάριτι χριστοῦ εἰς ἕτερον εὐαγγέλιον
verses.king_james: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:

verses.latin_vulgate: quod non est aliud, nisi sunt aliqui qui vos conturbant, et volunt convertere Evangelium Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλο εἰ μή τινές εἰσιν οἱ ταράσσοντες ὑμᾶς καὶ θέλοντες μεταστρέψαι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed licet nos aut angelus de cælo evangelizet vobis præterquam quod evangelizavimus vobis, anathema sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ καὶ ἐὰν ἡμεῖς ἢ ἄγγελος ἐξ οὐρανοῦ εὐαγγελίζηται ὑμῖν παρ᾽ ὃ εὐηγγελισάμεθα ὑμῖν ἀνάθεμα ἔστω
verses.king_james: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut prædiximus, et nunc iterum dico: si quis vobis evangelizaverit præter id quod accepistis, anathema sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς προειρήκαμεν καὶ ἄρτι πάλιν λέγω εἴ τις ὑμᾶς εὐαγγελίζεται παρ᾽ ὃ παρελάβετε ἀνάθεμα ἔστω
verses.king_james: As we said before, so say I now again, If any *man* preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Modo enim hominibus suadeo, an Deo? an quæro hominibus placere? si adhuc hominibus placerem, Christi servus non essem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρτι γὰρ ἀνθρώπους πείθω ἢ τὸν θεόν ἢ ζητῶ ἀνθρώποις ἀρέσκειν εἰ γὰρ ἔτι ἀνθρώποις ἤρεσκον χριστοῦ δοῦλος οὐκ ἂν ἤμην
verses.king_james: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Notum enim vobis facio, fratres, Evangelium, quod evangelizatum est a me, quia non est secundum hominem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γνωρίζω δὲ ὑμῖν ἀδελφοί τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τὸ εὐαγγελισθὲν ὑπ᾽ ἐμοῦ ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν κατὰ ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.

verses.latin_vulgate: neque enim ego ab homine accepi illud, neque didici, sed per revelationem Jesu Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ γὰρ ἐγὼ παρὰ ἀνθρώπου παρέλαβον αὐτό οὔτε ἐδιδάχθην ἀλλὰ δι᾽ ἀποκαλύψεως ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught *it*, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audistis enim conversationem meam aliquando in Judaismo: quoniam supra modum persequebar Ecclesiam Dei, et expugnabam illam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠκούσατε γὰρ τὴν ἐμὴν ἀναστροφήν ποτε ἐν τῷ ἰουδαϊσμῷ ὅτι καθ᾽ ὑπερβολὴν ἐδίωκον τὴν ἐκκλησίαν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἐπόρθουν αὐτήν
verses.king_james: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews' religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:

verses.latin_vulgate: et proficiebam in Judaismo supra multos coætaneos meos in genere meo, abundantius æmulator existens paternarum mearum traditionum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προέκοπτον ἐν τῷ ἰουδαϊσμῷ ὑπὲρ πολλοὺς συνηλικιώτας ἐν τῷ γένει μου περισσοτέρως ζηλωτὴς ὑπάρχων τῶν πατρικῶν μου παραδόσεων
verses.king_james: And profited in the Jews' religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem placuit ei, qui me segregavit ex utero matris meæ, et vocavit per gratiam suam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ εὐδόκησεν ὁ θεὸς ὁ ἀφορίσας με ἐκ κοιλίας μητρός μου καὶ καλέσας διὰ τῆς χάριτος αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother's womb, and called *me* by his grace,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut revelaret Filium suum in me, ut evangelizarem illum in gentibus: continuo non acquievi carni et sanguini,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκαλύψαι τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ ἐν ἐμοὶ ἵνα εὐαγγελίζωμαι αὐτὸν ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν εὐθέως οὐ προσανεθέμην σαρκὶ καὶ αἵματι
verses.king_james: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:

verses.latin_vulgate: neque veni Jerosolymam ad antecessores meos Apostolos: sed abii in Arabiam, et iterum reversus sum Damascum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ ἀνῆλθον εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα πρὸς τοὺς πρὸ ἐμοῦ ἀποστόλους ἀλλ᾽ ἀπῆλθον εἰς ἀραβίαν καὶ πάλιν ὑπέστρεψα εἰς δαμασκόν
verses.king_james: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.

verses.latin_vulgate: deinde post annos tres veni Jerosolymam videre Petrum, et mansi apud eum diebus quindecim:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕπειτα μετὰ ἔτη τρία ἀνῆλθον εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα ἱστορῆσαι πέτρον, καὶ ἐπέμεινα πρὸς αὐτὸν ἡμέρας δεκαπέντε
verses.king_james: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.

verses.latin_vulgate: alium autem Apostolorum vidi neminem, nisi Jacobum fratrem Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕτερον δὲ τῶν ἀποστόλων οὐκ εἶδον εἰ μὴ ἰάκωβον τὸν ἀδελφὸν τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord's brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ autem scribo vobis, ecce coram Deo, quia non mentior.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἃ δὲ γράφω ὑμῖν ἰδού, ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ ὅτι οὐ ψεύδομαι
verses.king_james: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deinde veni in partes Syriæ, et Ciliciæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔπειτα ἦλθον εἰς τὰ κλίματα τῆς συρίας καὶ τῆς κιλικίας
verses.king_james: Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;

verses.latin_vulgate: Eram autem ignotus facie ecclesiis Judææ, quæ erant in Christo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤμην δὲ ἀγνοούμενος τῷ προσώπῳ ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς ἰουδαίας ταῖς ἐν χριστῷ
verses.king_james: And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:

verses.latin_vulgate: tantum autem auditum habebant quoniam qui persequebatur nos aliquando, nunc evangelizat fidem, quam aliquando expugnabat:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μόνον δὲ ἀκούοντες ἦσαν ὅτι ὁ διώκων ἡμᾶς ποτε νῦν εὐαγγελίζεται τὴν πίστιν ἥν ποτε ἐπόρθει
verses.king_james: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.

verses.latin_vulgate: et in me clarificabant Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐδόξαζον ἐν ἐμοὶ τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: And they glorified God in me.
